pr.title: Replace raw 0x10 with JOBJ_HIDDEN in HSD_JObj flag calls
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Code-style PR replacing raw hidden-flag literals in HSD_JObj flag calls with the named JOBJ_HIDDEN macro across 505 call sites in 73 files. The PR body says the sweep was syntax-aware, limited to HSD_JObj{Set,Clear}Flags[All] flag arguments, left composite masks and other flag values untouched, and verified all 1046 compiled objects plus main.dol byte-identical by sha1.

what_changed: Replaced raw hidden-flag constants 0x10, 0x10U, 16, and 16U with JOBJ_HIDDEN when used as the flag argument to HSD_JObj{Set,Clear}Flags[All] calls.
Touched 505 call sites across 73 files according to the PR body.
Changed files span effect, game-mode, stage, interface, item, menu, and toy source paths.
Composite masks and other flag values were explicitly left untouched according to the PR body.
No semantic behavior change was claimed; the PR body reports all 1046 compiled objects byte-identical by per-object sha1 and main.dol sha1 unchanged.
smart_moves: Limited the replacement to the flag argument of HSD_JObj Set/Clear flag APIs rather than doing a blind textual replacement of all 0x10-like literals.
Kept composite masks and non-hidden flag values out of scope, reducing risk of changing intended bitmask semantics.
Used the named JOBJ_HIDDEN constant to make visibility toggles more readable while preserving generated code.
Reported broad byte-identity validation across compiled objects and main.dol for a large mechanical cleanup.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a raw numeric literal is a known API flag value, replacing it with the named macro can be a safe readability cleanup if the sweep is syntax-aware and limited to the relevant API argument.
For large mechanical style cleanups, preserve semantics by explicitly excluding composite masks and unrelated flag values, then verify byte identity.

naming_conventions.convention: Use JOBJ_HIDDEN instead of raw 0x10, 0x10U, 16, or 16U when that literal is the hidden flag argument to HSD_JObj{Set,Clear}Flags[All].

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Byte-identical macro substitution: replace numeric flag literals with an equivalent named macro and verify compiled output does not change.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:All 1046 compiled objects verified byte-identical before and after the change

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use syntax-aware matching for mass edits so only the intended API argument is changed.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text:Composite masks and other flag values are untouched

follow_up_queries: Find remaining HSD_JObjSetFlags or HSD_JObjClearFlags calls with raw 0x10/0x10U/16/16U flag arguments after PR #2675.
Search for composite HSD_JObj flag masks containing JOBJ_HIDDEN to ensure they were intentionally excluded or later cleaned up.
Search PR #2670 for the parent context of the JOBJ_HIDDEN source cleanup split.
Check whether decomp standards should mention named HSD_JObj flag macros for raw flag literals.

notes: PR body directly states scope, number of call sites, number of files, excluded masks, and byte-identity validation.
Diff excerpt shows representative replacements from raw 0x10/0x10U to JOBJ_HIDDEN in HSD_JObj flag calls.
No review comments or issue comments were present in the slice, so review intent beyond merge metadata is not inferred.
Raw diff is truncated in the loaded slice, but changed_files.jsonl provides the complete changed-file list.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use named HSD_JObj hidden flag macro for raw hidden-bit literals in JObj flag calls
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2675 supports that raw 0x10/0x10U/16/16U literals used as the hidden-flag argument to HSD_JObj{Set,Clear}Flags[All] can be replaced with JOBJ_HIDDEN as a byte-identical readability cleanup when the edit is syntax-aware and excludes composite masks or unrelated flag values.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer named HSD_JObj flag macros over raw hidden flag literals
source_update_candidates.text: For HSD_JObj Set/Clear flag calls, use JOBJ_HIDDEN instead of raw 0x10/0x10U/16/16U when the literal is the hidden flag argument. Do this with syntax-aware edits, do not rewrite composite masks or unrelated flags without evidence, and verify byte identity for large mechanical sweeps.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded decomp standards cover named fields/macros generally but do not specifically encode this HSD_JObj hidden-flag naming convention.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every 0x10 literal in these files is JOBJ_HIDDEN; the PR evidence is limited to specific HSD_JObj flag-call arguments.
Do not infer that composite masks containing the hidden bit should always be rewritten; the PR body says composite masks were left untouched.
Do not use this PR to claim new function matches; it is presented as a byte-identical code-style cleanup.
